chunks.text: Tz TezDel Complete Startup Blueprint Hyperlocal Food & Grocery Delivery — Bhubaneswar, Odisha Market Bhubaneswar Model Hyperlocal Q-Commerce Phase 1 30 Days Edge Zero Commission Table of Contents 01 Executive Summary3 02 Market & Competitive Landscape4 03 TezDel's Five Competitive Weapons5 04 Business Model & Revenue Streams7 05 Unit Economics8 06 Operational Model9 07 Home Chef Network10 08 Kirana Partner Program11 09 Technology Stack12 10 Go-to-Market Strategy13 11 30-60-90 Day Roadmap14 12 Financial Projections15 13 Funding & Capital Requirements16 14 ONDC Integration Plan17 15 Team & Hiring Plan18 16 Risk Analysis & Mitigation19 17 Vision & Long-Term Roadmap20

01 — EXECUTIVE SUMMARY What is TezDel? TezDel (Tez = fast in Odia/Hindi + Del = deliver) is a hyperlocal food and grocery delivery platform built specifically for Bhubaneswar. Unlike national players who simply replicate their metro model in Tier-2 cities, TezDel is designed from the ground up for the city — leveraging existing neighbourhood infrastructure, authentic Odia cuisine, and deep community trust as its primary competitive moats. The name is deliberate. 'Tez' is instantly understood in both Odia and Hindi — it means fast. 'Del' signals delivery. Together: fast delivery, with a local soul. Short, pronounceable, and memorable across every language spoken in Bhubaneswar. Core Mission Statement To make every neighbourhood in Bhubaneswar self-sufficient through a trusted, fast delivery network — powered by home chefs, local kiranas, and community captains — not dark stores and corporate warehouses. 20 min Target Delivery Time ₹0 Commission to Restaurants ₹999/mo Flat Restaurant Fee Day 1 ONDC Integration Why Bhubaneswar, Why Now? Bhubaneswar is experiencing a perfect storm of conditions that make it ideal for a hyperlocal delivery startup: Rapidly growing young professional population in Infocity, Patia, and Nayapalli corridors

High density of IT workers and college students with disposable income and mobile-first habits Severe underrepresentation of authentic Odia food in existing delivery platforms Local restaurant owners who are actively frustrated by Swiggy/Zomato commission structures Tier-2 economics: breakeven at just 800 daily orders versus 1,300 in metros Flashnow operating as the only serious local competitor — and showing the market demand is real

02 — MARKET & COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE The Battlefield India's quick-commerce market hit $5.38 billion in 2025, growing at 17% — the fastest rate globally. Blinkit commands over 50% national market share and entered Bhubaneswar in January 2025. But massive capital creates massive blind spots. Here is exactly how to read the competitive landscape: Competitive Matrix Player Presence Commission Odia Food Home Chefs Kirana Model Capital Flashnow Bhubaneswar 20–25% Limited None Unknown Small Blinkit Entered Jan 2025 High None None No (dark stores) ₹4,300 Cr+ Swiggy Metro-first 22–30% Partial None No Very High Zomato Metro-first 22–30% Partial None No Very High TezDel ★ Bhubaneswar-first ₹999/mo flat Core identity Core model Core model Low & lean The Critical Insight Blinkit spent ₹4,300 crore and still cannot cook your grandmother's pakhala. Capital buys warehouses and delivery bikes. It cannot buy neighbourhood trust, home-cooked authenticity, or a local restaurant owner's loyalty. These are TezDel's permanent advantages — structural moats that increase in value as the market grows. Flashnow — Your Immediate Target Flashnow is the immediate competitor to displace. As a local Bhubaneswar app, it is proving the market exists — but its model has key weaknesses you can exploit:

Standard commission model frustrating local restaurants (same problem as Swiggy/Zomato at smaller scale) No home chef network — purely restaurant-focused No kirana grocery integration at community level No cultural or food identity — generic delivery app positioning No ONDC infrastructure — vulnerable to being bypassed as ONDC grows TezDel's strategy is not to fight Flashnow on speed or marketing spend. It is to make Flashnow irrelevant by offering restaurant partners a model so superior they actively switch — and offering consumers a product Flashnow simply doesn't have: real Odia home cooking.

03 — COMPETITIVE ADVANTAGES TezDel's Five Competitive Weapons These five structural advantages cannot be replicated by national players. Each one compounds over time and becomes more valuable as TezDel grows. Weapon 1: Odia-First Home Chef Network The single biggest gap in Bhubaneswar's delivery market: no platform serves authentic Odia food. At all. Pakhala, besara, dalma, macha besara, chenna poda, tarkari, dalma — the food working professionals and college students in Bhubaneswar actually crave — is completely absent from Swiggy and Zomato's city listings. These platforms list the same 50 restaurants serving biryani and pizza. TezDel builds a vetted network of home cooks (primarily women working from home) who prepare and list their daily menu. This is not just a feature — it becomes TezDel's brand identity. 'TezDel is where you get real Odia food' is a word-of-mouth story that spreads naturally in a city with deep cultural pride. Home Chef Program Details: Verified, FSSAI-compliant home cooks onboarded through a structured audit process Each chef lists their daily menu by 9 AM; orders accepted until 12 PM for lunch, 5 PM for dinner TezDel provides packaging material, food-safe containers, and labeling in first 3 months

Chef earns 80% of listed price — higher than any restaurant on any platform Monthly 'Star Chef' recognition program with bonus and featured placement Phase 1 target: 20 verified home chefs in Patia and Nayapalli Phase 2 target: 100 home chefs across 5 zones by Month 6 Phase 3 target: 300+ home chefs citywide including specialty (diet, diabetic, Jain) by Year 1 Weapon 2: Kirana-as-Dark-Store (Zero Capex) Blinkit spends approximately ₹80 lakh per dark store. TezDel spends ₹0. Rather than building or leasing expensive micro-fulfillment centers, TezDel partners with existing neighbourhood kirana shops as order fulfilment nodes. They already have inventory, already know their customers, and already operate 6 days a week. TezDel adds them as digital-order-receiving partners. Kirana Partner Benefits: Guaranteed additional orders through TezDel's app and ONDC network Digital payment processing and instant settlement — no cash handling No upfront fee to join; TezDel earns a small logistics margin per order Monthly performance bonus for top-rated kirana partners Access to TezDel's demand analytics — know what your locality is ordering before they order TezDel Benefits: Immediate geographic coverage across 10+ zones with zero real estate cost

Authentic local assortment (kiranas stock what their neighbourhood actually buys) Built-in community trust — the kirana owner IS the neighbourhood This model is impossible for Blinkit to copy — it would cannibalize their own dark stores Weapon 3: Zero Commission Restaurant Model Swiggy and Zomato take 22–30% of every order. On a ₹300 order that's ₹90 gone before the restaurant pays rent, staff, or ingredients. TezDel charges restaurants a flat ₹999/month subscription — regardless of order volume. The math is immediately obvious to every restaurant owner: Scenario Zomato/Swiggy Cost TezDel Cost 50 orders/month @ ₹300 avg ₹3,750 (25% commission) ₹999 flat 100 orders/month @ ₹300 avg ₹7,500 (25% commission) ₹999 flat 200 orders/month @ ₹300 avg ₹15,000 (25% commission) ₹999 flat 500 orders/month @ ₹300 avg ₹37,500 (25% commission) ₹999 flat This creates passionate restaurant partners who will actively recommend TezDel to their dine-in customers, display TezDel stickers at their entrance, and prioritize TezDel orders over Swiggy/Zomato when busy. You turn every restaurant into a sales team. Weapon 4: Neighbourhood Captain Model

Rather than anonymous gig workers, TezDel assigns one dedicated part-time 'Neighbourhood Captain' per housing colony or mohalla. This person lives in the area, knows residents by name, and handles deliveries within a 1–2 km radius for 3–4 hours daily. Captain Economics: Earnings: ₹8,000–15,000/month for part-time work (3–4 hours/day) Works alongside their existing job or as a primary income source Trusted figure — known face in the colony, not a stranger handing over a bag Trust dramatically reduces cancellations, wrong-delivery incidents, and returns Word-of-mouth: captains naturally tell their neighbours about TezDel Phase 1: 10 captains covering 10 housing colonies in Patia and Nayapalli Weapon 5: ONDC Integration from Day One ONDC (Open Network for Digital Commerce) is India's government-backed open protocol for e-commerce — think of it as the UPI of delivery. By building TezDel as an ONDC seller-app from the first day of launch, every restaurant and home chef on TezDel becomes discoverable across all ONDC buyer apps: WAAYU, Paytm, PhonePe, Magicpin, and any future app that joins the network. TezDel functions as both a consumer app AND as infrastructure that others build on Restaurants gain multi-platform exposure without paying multiple platforms

TezDel earns logistics and seller-app fees from ONDC transactions — even when orders arrive through other buyer apps As ONDC grows (projected to handle 50%+ of food orders by 2027), TezDel's network becomes more valuable, not less WAAYU launched India's first zero-commission food delivery on ONDC in December 2024 — TezDel follows the same model with local execution advantage

04 — BUSINESS MODEL Revenue Streams TezDel has six revenue streams across three phases. None of them squeeze restaurant margins. The model is designed so restaurant success = TezDel success. Phase 1 Revenue (Months 1–6) Revenue Stream Structure Phase 1 Target Restaurant Subscription ₹999/month per restaurant — flat fee, no commission 50 restaurants = ₹49,950/month Delivery Fee (Consumer) ₹20–40 per delivery charged to customer based on distance 500 orders/day = ₹10K–20K daily Home Chef Commission TezDel retains 20% of home chef order value 20 chefs × ₹1,500 avg GMV = ₹6,000/day Phase 2 Revenue (Months 7–12) Revenue Stream Structure Target TezPass Subscription ₹149/month consumer subscription — free delivery + exclusive benefits 2,000 subscribers = ₹2.98L/month Featured Listings / Ads ₹2,000–5,000/month for featured placement in app 30 featured restaurants = ₹90K–1.5L/month Phase 3 Revenue (Year 2+) Revenue Stream Structure Target ONDC Transaction Fee Seller-app fee on ONDC-routed orders from other buyer apps Passive revenue as ONDC scales nationally B2B Corporate Meals Bulk daily lunch contracts with IT companies in Infocity/Patia 10 corporate contracts = ₹3–5L/month

05 — UNIT ECONOMICS The Numbers Per Order At steady-state, modelling 500 daily orders across 10 zones of Bhubaneswar. Tier-2 city economics are fundamentally better than metros — breakeven at 800 daily orders versus 1,300 in Bengaluru or Mumbai. Per Order P&L Line Item Amount Type Average Order Value (AOV) ₹280 — Delivery fee (collected from consumer) + ₹30 Revenue Platform fee allocation (from ₹999 subscription, per order) + ₹5 Revenue Total Revenue Per Order ₹35 — Delivery captain cost (₹8K/month ÷ ~120 orders/month) - ₹18 Cost App & payment processing (Razorpay ~1.8%) - ₹5 Cost Customer acquisition amortization - ₹4 Cost Contribution Margin Per Order ₹8 Profit 500 daily orders × ₹8 contribution = ₹4,000/day contribution margin = ₹1.2L/month before fixed overhead. At 800 orders/day TezDel covers fixed overhead and turns profitable at the operations level.

06 — OPERATIONAL MODEL How TezDel Operates Daily TezDel's operational model is asset-light by design. No dark stores. No owned vehicles. No large warehouses. The city's existing infrastructure — kiranas and home cooks — becomes TezDel's supply chain. A Typical Day in TezDel's Operations 6:00 AM — Home chefs confirm their daily menu via TezDel app. Menu listed live by 9 AM. 8:00 AM — Kirana partners open and confirm inventory levels in the system. Low-stock alerts sent automatically. 9:00 AM — Neighbourhood captains check in for morning shift. Route optimization pushed to their app. 10:00 AM onwards — Orders begin flowing from app, WhatsApp ordering link, and ONDC buyer apps. 12:00–2:00 PM — Peak lunch window. Captains handle highest delivery volume. Home chef orders prioritized. 5:00–9:00 PM — Evening/dinner peak. Restaurant orders dominate. Grocery top-up orders from kirana partners. 9:00 PM — Operations wind down. Daily metrics reviewed. Captain earnings settled. 10:00 PM — Next-day chef menus submitted. Kirana inventory sync for morning. Delivery Zones — Phase 1 Phase 1 launches in two high-density zones before citywide expansion: Zone Key Localities Target Captains Target Restaurants Zone 1 — Patia Patia, Chandrasekharpur, Infocity 5 25 Zone 2 — Nayapalli
Nayapalli, Saheed Nagar, VSS Nagar 5 25 Zone 3–10 (Phase 2) Unit 1–9, Khandagiri, Baramunda, Old Town... +20 +150

07 — HOME CHEF NETWORK Building the Chef Network The home chef network is TezDel's most powerful differentiator. It creates a product that no competitor can copy, generates authentic word-of-mouth growth, and empowers women entrepreneurs in Bhubaneswar. Onboarding Process Discovery: Field team visits housing colonies and women's self-help groups (SHGs). WhatsApp referral from existing chefs. Screening: Kitchen inspection for basic hygiene. FSSAI registration assistance provided by TezDel. Training: 2-hour onboarding session — app usage, packaging standards, food safety, pricing guidance. Soft Launch: Chef gets 10 free orders promoted by TezDel to their zone. Builds reviews and confidence. Full Listing: Chef goes live on TezDel app and ONDC network. Profile includes story, specialty dishes, and photos. Ongoing Support: Monthly chef community meetup. WhatsApp group for real-time support. Dedicated chef success manager. Chef Economics A home chef cooking lunch for 20 orders per day: Revenue: 20 orders × ₹120 avg tiffin price = ₹2,400/day TezDel's 20% cut: ₹480 Chef earns: ₹1,920/day from home, without any commute or office hours Monthly earnings: ₹40,000–57,600/month (22–25 cooking days) Food cost at 35%: ₹672/day → Net chef income: ₹1,248/day or ₹28,000–35,000/month

This is a transformative income opportunity for homemakers in Bhubaneswar. The story sells itself. Odia Cuisine Menu Categories Daily Tiffin: Pakhala, dalma, saga bhaja, macha jhola, tarkari — rotating daily menu Special Weekend: Chenna poda, kheer, rasabali, chhena jhili — weekend-only items Diet & Health: Low-oil, low-salt versions for the IT professional segment Festival Specials: Raja, Durga Puja, Diwali, Rath Yatra — limited menu with pre-orders Regional Specialties: Koraput, Sambalpur, Cuttack styles — diversity of Odia cuisine

08 — KIRANA PARTNER PROGRAM Kirana Partner Program TezDel's grocery supply model completely bypasses the need for warehouse investment. The neighbourhood kirana IS the dark store — stocked, staffed, and trusted. Kirana Selection Criteria Located inside or adjacent to a housing colony (not on a main road) Minimum 5 years of operation (proven neighbourhood trust) Has a smartphone and is comfortable with WhatsApp orders Stocks essential FMCG, staples, dairy, and fresh produce Willing to dedicate a small counter space for TezDel order staging Technology Integration TezDel provides a simple Android tablet or configures their existing phone with the Kirana Partner app Orders appear as WhatsApp-style notifications. Pick, pack, hand to captain. Real-time inventory sync — kirana marks items as unavailable; app auto-removes from customer listings Weekly digital settlement statement with GST-ready invoicing Monthly sales analytics showing which products are trending in their zone Phase 1 Grocery Categories Daily essentials: milk, bread, eggs, curd, butter, paneer Staples: rice, dal, atta, oil, sugar, salt, spices Packaged goods: biscuits, noodles, snacks, beverages Personal care: soap, shampoo, toothpaste, sanitary items Baby care: diapers, formula, baby food

09 — TECHNOLOGY STACK Technology Architecture TezDel's tech stack is built lean-first. Phase 1 uses existing tools and no-code platforms to get to market in 30 days. Custom development happens as revenue validates the business. Phase 1: MVP Stack (Days 0–30) Consumer Ordering: WhatsApp Business API + Google Forms for order intake (zero app development cost) Restaurant & Chef Portal: Glide or AppSheet no-code app for menu management Captain App: ShareTribe or custom WhatsApp bot for order assignment Payments: Razorpay payment links sent via WhatsApp Tracking: Google Maps shared location link from captain's phone Database: Airtable or Google Sheets for order management in Phase 1 Phase 2: App Launch (Months 3–6) Consumer App: React Native (single codebase for Android + iOS) — Android-first given Bhubaneswar market Backend: Node.js + PostgreSQL on AWS or Google Cloud Real-time Tracking: Firebase Realtime Database for live order updates Payment: Razorpay full integration with UPI, cards, wallets, and BNPL Captain App: React Native companion app with route optimization via Google Maps API Kirana Portal: Web app for inventory management and order receiving Phase 3: ONDC + AI (Months 7–12)

ONDC Seller App Integration: Build to Beckn protocol specifications; become registered ONDC seller-app Demand Forecasting: ML model predicting peak demand by zone and time window Menu Personalization: AI recommendations based on order history and locality preferences Route Optimization: Custom routing algorithm for multi-stop captain deliveries Chef Menu Suggester: AI tool that recommends daily menus to home chefs based on demand patterns

10 — GO-TO-MARKET STRATEGY How TezDel Wins Customers TezDel's GTM is radically offline and community-first. No paid digital ads in Phase 1. Every rupee goes into physical presence, supply side quality, and referral incentives. Phase 1 GTM — Offline First (Days 1–30) Colony-by-colony blitz: Personal visit to 20 housing societies. Demo the product at the gate. First 50 orders free. Restaurant partner events: Host a 'Freedom from Commission' tea session with local restaurant association. Sign 20 restaurants at the event. Home chef recruitment drive: Visit women's SHGs in Patia and Nayapalli. Co-host with a credible NGO or women's group. Kirana partner walks: Walk into ground-floor kiranas in target colonies. 15-minute pitch: more customers, no cost. Referral launch: Every user gets a unique referral link. ₹50 credit for referrer, ₹50 discount for new user. WhatsApp community groups: Create zone-specific WhatsApp groups. Share daily home chef menus. Build engagement before the app launches. Phase 2 GTM — Digital Layer (Months 2–6) Instagram & Facebook: Daily 'Chef of the Day' reels featuring home chefs and their signature dishes — authentic content, no ads needed YouTube Shorts: 60-second videos of Odia food being cooked and delivered — massive organic reach potential

Google My Business: Optimize for 'food delivery Bhubaneswar' and 'Odia food delivery' search terms Partnered content: Work with Odia food bloggers and influencers — authentic endorsements, not paid ads Office ordering: Visit IT companies in Infocity. Offer a bulk lunch trial week. Seal corporate contracts. Retention Strategy TezPass subscription (₹149/month) creates habitual daily ordering behaviour 'Odia Food Calendar' — push notifications for festival special menus 3 days in advance Loyalty points: 1 point per ₹10 spent, redeemable for free delivery or discounts Captain relationship: Regular customers know their captain by name — this alone drives retention Monthly 'Neighbourhood Favourites' list: Top-rated dishes in each zone, published on WhatsApp and Instagram

11 — ROADMAP 30 – 60 – 90 Day Roadmap Days 1–30: Foundation Sprint Goal: First 500 orders. Supply side (restaurants, chefs, kiranas) built. 10 captains live. WhatsApp ordering working. Week Actions Week 1 Register company (Pvt Ltd). Set up Razorpay. Create WhatsApp Business. Design branding. Print 500 flyers. Week 2 Walk Patia zone: sign 10 restaurants, 5 home chefs, 3 kiranas. Recruit 5 captains. Train everyone. Week 3 Soft launch Patia zone only. First 50 orders free. Collect feedback. Fix issues daily. Week 4 Walk Nayapalli zone. Sign 10 more restaurants, 5 chefs, 3 kiranas, 5 captains. Expand ordering. Days 31–60: Growth Sprint Goal: 200+ daily orders. 50 restaurants live. 20 home chefs. App MVP launched. First corporate client. Launch TezDel Android app (React Native MVP — order, track, pay) Run first referral campaign — ₹50 credit for every successful referral Approach 5 IT companies in Infocity for corporate lunch pilot Begin FSSAI registrations for all home chefs — TezDel handles the paperwork First 'Chef Spotlight' Instagram reel — document the viral potential Sign first 50th restaurant; host a partner celebration event Days 61–90: Scale Sprint Goal: 400+ daily orders. 100 restaurants. 50 home chefs. ONDC integration begun. TezPass launched.

Expand to 2 new zones (Saheed Nagar, Khandagiri) Launch TezPass subscription (₹149/month) to top 500 users first Begin ONDC seller-app registration and protocol integration Hire first full-time operations manager and tech developer First media coverage: pitch story to Sambad, OTV, and Pragativadi about the home chef model Raise pre-seed round of ₹50–75 lakhs based on 90-day traction data

12 — FINANCIAL PROJECTIONS 12-Month Financial Projections Metric Month 1 Month 3 Month 6 Month 12 Daily Orders 50 200 500 1,200 Restaurants 20 50 120 280 Home Chefs 10 25 80 200 Kirana Partners 6 15 35 80 Monthly GMV ₹4.2L ₹16.8L ₹42L ₹1.0 Cr Monthly Revenue ₹55K ₹2.2L ₹5.5L ₹13.2L Monthly Costs ₹1.5L ₹3.0L ₹4.8L ₹10.0L Net P&L -₹95K -₹80K +₹70K +₹3.2L Breakeven point: approximately Month 5–6 at ~500 daily orders. Year 1 cumulative investment required: ₹35–50 lakhs. Month 12 run rate revenue: ₹13.2L/month = ₹1.58 Cr annualized.

13 — FUNDING & CAPITAL REQUIREMENTS Capital Plan Phase 1: Bootstrap (₹10–15 Lakhs) Founder capital or family/friends round ₹3L — App development (MVP React Native) ₹2L — Marketing (flyers, events, referral credits) ₹2L — Captain hiring and training ₹2L — Packaging materials for home chefs ₹2L — Legal, FSSAI registration, accounting, company setup ₹2–4L — Working capital buffer Pre-Seed Round: ₹50–75 Lakhs (Month 3–4) Raised after demonstrating 200+ daily orders and 50+ restaurant partners. Use of funds: ₹20L — Tech platform upgrade (full app, ONDC integration, tracking) ₹15L — Marketing and user acquisition campaign ₹10L — Team hiring (ops manager, chef success manager, sales) ₹10L — Captain network expansion to 4 new zones ₹10L — Working capital and contingency Seed Round: ₹2–3 Crores (Month 9–12) Raised after demonstrating 800+ daily orders, positive contribution margin, and ONDC live. Use of funds: Citywide expansion across all 15+ zones of Bhubaneswar Product excellence: ML recommendations, real-time tracking, chef analytics Team scale: 25–30 employees including tech team, operations, and city leads Expansion planning: Cuttack and Sambalpur market entry preparation

14 — ONDC INTEGRATION ONDC Integration Plan ONDC (Open Network for Digital Commerce) is the most important strategic lever for TezDel's long-term competitive position. Understanding and executing this correctly separates TezDel from every competitor in Bhubaneswar. What is ONDC and Why It Matters ONDC is a government-backed open protocol (like UPI for payments, but for commerce) that allows any seller-app and buyer-app to communicate — without being on the same platform. When TezDel is a registered ONDC seller-app: A customer on PhonePe can order from a TezDel-listed restaurant without having TezDel's app installed A Paytm user can order from a TezDel-listed home chef through Paytm's ONDC buyer interface TezDel gets order fulfillment revenue from every such transaction Restaurants on TezDel effectively get listed on 10+ platforms simultaneously at no extra cost Implementation Roadmap Month 1–2: Register with ONDC network. Study Beckn protocol documentation. Month 3–4: Build seller-app to ONDC protocol spec (can use ONDC reference apps to accelerate). Month 5–6: Integration testing with ONDC sandbox. Get certified as ONDC seller-app. Month 6+: Go live on ONDC. All TezDel restaurants and chefs discoverable across ONDC buyer apps.

Month 9+: Build ONDC buyer-app layer so TezDel users can also order from other ONDC sellers. Long-term vision: TezDel becomes the dominant ONDC seller-app infrastructure for all of Odisha — the backbone through which every local restaurant in the state reaches digital customers, regardless of which buyer app they use.

15 — TEAM & HIRING Team & Hiring Plan Founding Team (Day 1) Role Requirement Responsibilities Founder / CEO You (visionary) Strategy, fundraising, partnerships, brand, restaurant BD Co-founder / Ops Operations background Captain management, kirana onboarding, daily operations Tech Co-founder / Dev React Native developer App development, ONDC integration, backend Month 3–6 Hires Operations Manager (₹30–40K/month) — day-to-day delivery operations, captain scheduling Chef Success Manager (₹20–25K/month) — home chef onboarding, quality, menu coaching Sales Executive (₹18–22K/month + incentives) — restaurant and kirana acquisition Customer Support (₹15K/month) — WhatsApp support, order resolution Month 9–12 Hires Zone Managers × 4 (₹25–35K/month each) — one per major delivery zone Marketing Manager (₹35–45K/month) — digital content, partnerships, growth Backend Developer (₹50–70K/month) — ONDC, API, data engineering Finance & Accounting (₹25–35K/month) — investor reporting, GST compliance

16 — RISK ANALYSIS Risks & Mitigation Level Risk Impact Mitigation HIGH Blinkit aggressive price war / heavy discounting in Bhubaneswar Customer churn to free/discounted Blinkit orders Focus on Odia home food — a category Blinkit cannot enter. Build TezPass loyalty before Blinkit discounts begin. HIGH Home chef quality inconsistency leading to bad reviews Reputational damage, customer loss Strict audit process. Rating system with automatic pause at below 3.5 stars. Monthly quality workshops. MEDIUM Delivery captain attrition / reliability issues Delivery failures, customer complaints Strong earnings + community identity. Backup captain roster. Zone manager oversight. MEDIUM Restaurant partners return to Swiggy/Zomato after initial switch Supply side erosion Annual contracts with signing bonus. Make TezDel orders 30%+ of restaurant revenue — makes switching painful. MEDIUM ONDC regulatory changes or protocol shifts Tech rework required Maintain proprietary app as primary channel. ONDC as additive layer, not sole infrastructure. LOW Food safety incident at a home chef kitchen PR and legal exposure FSSAI compliance for all chefs. Food safety audit every 6 months. ₹2L food safety liability insurance. LOW Flashnow raises capital and matches TezDel's model Increased local competition
18-month head start on chef network and kirana relationships. Community trust is the moat — not easily funded.

17 — VISION & LONG-TERM ROADMAP The Long-Term Vision Year 1: Own Bhubaneswar 1,200+ daily orders across 15 zones 280+ restaurants, 200 home chefs, 80 kirana partners ONDC seller-app live — discoverable on PhonePe, Paytm, WAAYU TezPass at 5,000+ subscribers ₹1.58 Cr annualized revenue, EBITDA positive Year 2: Own Odisha Expand to Cuttack (Bhubaneswar's twin city) — same playbook, 60 days to launch Enter Sambalpur, Berhampur, Rourkela — major Odisha cities Launch 'TezDel Home Kitchen' private label — Odia ready meals for diaspora delivery Become the ONDC seller-app of record for all of Odisha ₹10 Cr+ ARR target Year 3–5: East India Expansion Apply the Odia-food-first playbook to other culturally distinct markets: Guwahati (Assamese food), Patna (Bihari), Ranchi (Jharkhand) Each city gets a local home chef network and zero-commission restaurant model TezDel becomes the hyperlocal delivery brand for Eastern India that national players cannot displace Potential acqui-hire target for Blinkit/Swiggy — or IPO-readiness as the ONDC infrastructure leader

The ultimate vision: TezDel is the platform through which every grandmother who cooks extraordinary local food, every neighbourhood kirana that knows its customers, and every local restaurant that deserves better margins — connects with the city they serve. Fast. Trusted. Local. TezDel — Tez Delivery, Deep Roots. Bhubaneswar's own. Built for the city. Built to last.
